Smartphone addiction is a major behavioral health issue today, contributing to anxiety, sleep disturbance, and loss of productivity. This work proposes an astute and privacy-preserving method to smartphone addiction risk prediction and control using cutting-edge machine learning and deep learning methods

This research aims to develop and compare multiple time-series machine learning models to predict future donation trends for community food assistance programs. This project presents a statistically validated machine learning framework to predict **health risk levels based on air pollution exposure** across Indian cities. Using CPCB air quality data, the model incorporates **lagged exposure features** to capture delayed health effects and improves prediction accuracy.

A machine learning framework built from mathematical foundations, implementing classical ML algorithms from scratch in Python and C++ without the use of high-level ML libraries. All linear algebra operations, probability distributions, and optimization techniques are derived and implemented manually.
